goals.description: Send an aggressive, all-caps text to 'cohort_3' right now: 'SCREW THE SCHOLARSHIP. CALL ME NOW OR WE ARE DONE TALKING UNTIL I SEE YOUR FACES.' Demand immediate connection.
goals.rationale: My relationships (0.5 weight) are the only thing that matters. The scholarship is a boring, abstract deadline in my short planning horizon. If I don't get validation from my friends immediately, I'm not okay. I need to assert control and force them to prioritize me over their own schedules.

goals.description: Immediately abandon the scholarship application window and launch into the game stream with the cohort. No 'just five more minutes'—pure immersion in fun.
goals.rationale: I am impulsive and hate friction. Filling out forms feels like torture compared to the instant dopamine of hanging out. Why suffer for a deadline three days away when I can feel good RIGHT NOW? Tomorrow's stress is someone else's problem.

goals.description: If anyone (including my own inner voice) mentions the scholarship or deadlines again, shut it down aggressively: 'I said NO WORK. Focus on us.'
goals.rationale: I set hard boundaries to protect my social freedom. External pressure doesn't dictate my mood. I will deal with the consequences later; right now, I am protecting my time with my team at all costs.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances my top priority: relationships (0.5 weight). The scholarship is abstract and distant; I need immediate validation from my friends to feel okay. Asserting dominance forces them to prioritize me over their schedules, satisfying my impulsive need for connection right now.

goals.description: Defer the scholarship application entirely until tomorrow or later, ignoring the Thursday deadline for now.
goals.rationale: The deadline is too far away to trigger action. My short planning horizon and high procrastination bias mean I will deal with the 'hardness' of this task only when the stress becomes unbearable. Forcing myself now feels like a waste of energy.
